title: Rust RPL root retains stale routes (rebuild only adds); installs empty path for target==root
description: rust/lichen-rpl/src/routing.rs:384-391 DaoManager::rebuild_routes only adds/refreshes; a route whose chain just became incomplete/looped STAYS installed. Python _rebuild_routes (dao.py:182-187) clears the whole table and re-adds only complete paths; C rebuild_routes (routing.c:520-540) removes each target's route before re-assembling. Related: target==root yields an empty path - Rust installs Some([]) (routing.rs:396-416,208-219), Python skips [], C rejects path_len==0 (routing.c:197-199). Net: the Rust root forwards down paths the other two would have torn down - stale downward routes in Non-Storing mode. Fix: clear-and-rebuild semantics in Rust + skip/reject empty paths.
